risk_ordered_items.risk_reason: Defines the contract between altered/unsaved order state, shared menu navigation, pending destination handling, and the existing Order Changed modal flow.
Complex shared implementation that may affect normal menu navigation and existing dirty-state middleware/dialog behavior across PEaRLS apps.
Destination-specific validation for Triage Worklist may expose gaps in shared menu URL/configuration handling.
Destination-specific validation for Specimen Accessioning may expose gaps in app-shell menu routing behavior.
End-to-end regression coverage depends on the completed generic and destination-specific behaviors; early execution would be blocked by missing implementation.

none.safety_rationale: No UoWs were marked parallel-safe. T3 and T4 both depend on T2 and appear superficially parallel, but they likely modify shared menu/header configuration, dialog test harness selectors, and related component tests, making sequential execution safer with lower merge-conflict risk.

librarian_queries.query: Scoped lessons and scheduling knowledge for assignment-agent creating execution schedule for change 5035632.
librarian_queries.answer_summary: No assignment-agent lessons applied. Librarian guidance emphasized serial dependency ordering, avoiding unsafe phase-boundary parallelism, preserving scope guardrails, and recording the full-confidence query in the output artifact.

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: A five-batch serial schedule was chosen because T1 defines the implementation contract, T2 implements shared dirty-page menu interception, T3 and T4 validate named destination paths that likely share files and test support, and T5 depends on both destination-specific flows for app-level regression coverage. This approach favors dependency correctness and merge safety over speculative parallelism.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Parallelize UOW-003 and UOW-004 after UOW-002.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Although their task dependencies both point only to T2, both destination tasks likely touch the same shared menu/header configuration, dialog selectors, and component test harnesses, so parallel execution would create avoidable merge and logic-integration risk.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Move UOW-005 earlier as a test-first regression task.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: The workflow does not include a separate automated test-writing stage, and T5 explicitly depends on T3 and T4 being complete.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Collapse destination-specific work into a single implementation UoW.
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: The assignment stage must schedule only the UoWs present in tasks.yaml and preserve task-to-UoW traceability.

knowledge_gaps: The runner-supplied target repo path contains a literal '~/'; intake identified /Users/mckerracher.joshua/Code/mcs-products-mono-ui as the likely resolved repository path, but implementation agents should confirm before editing source.

The story image for the Order Changed modal was preserved as an attachment reference but was not visually transcribed; implementation should rely on the existing in-repo Order Changed modal pattern and explicit story constraint.
Exact files to be modified by each UoW are not listed in tasks.yaml, so parallel-safety assessment is conservative.
The librarian scheduling summary referenced T1 through T4, while tasks.yaml contains T1 through T5; this schedule follows the authoritative tasks.yaml scope and includes T5.

tool_anomalies.anomaly: Returned full-confidence guidance with a serial T1->T4 summary, but the provided tasks.yaml contains T5. The schedule includes all five tasks to preserve complete traceability.
